mission.title: argo-workflows: Role-based Access Controls for SSO
mission.description: Role-based Access Controls for SSO. Requested by 72+ users.

steps.title: Check current argo-workflows deployment
steps.description:
Verify your argo-workflows version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n argo-workflows -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo-workflows
helm list -n argo-workflows 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working argo-workflows installation.

steps.title: Review argo-workflows configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant argo-workflows configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n argo-workflows -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo-workflows
kubectl get configmap -n argo-workflows -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=argo-workflows
```
Currently any user that logs in using SSO escalates to the `argo-server` service account. We would like other options.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Role-based Access Controls for SSO
steps.description:
Configure RBAC for SSO using Casbin policy in the workflow-controller-configmap. This treats the URL as the object and the HTTP method as the action.
```yaml
[request_definition]
r = sub, obj, act

[policy_definition]
p = sub, obj, act

[policy_effect]
e = some(where (p.eft == allow))

[matchers]
m = r.sub == p.sub && keyMatch(r.obj, p.obj) && (r.act == p.act || p.act == "*")
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n argo-workflows -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo-workflows
kubectl get events -n argo-workflows --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Role-based Access Controls for SSO" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: Implemented RBAC for SSO using Casbin. The URL is treated as the object and HTTP method as the action. Users can configure rules in the workflow-controller-configmap to map SSO groups to service accounts.
codeSnippets:
[request_definition]
r = sub, obj, act

[policy_definition]
p = sub, obj, act

[policy_effect]
e = some(where (p.eft == allow))

[matchers]
m = r.sub == p.sub && keyMatch(r.obj, p.obj) && (r.act == p.act || p.act == "*")

kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: workflow-controller-configmap
data:
  rbac: 
    # to configure the default account to use:
    defaultServiceAccount: my-sa
    # use the first matching rule
    rules:
    - groups: 
        anyOf:
        - my-oidc-group
       serviceAccount:
         name: my-sa

argo version
argo: v3.4.0
  BuildDate: 2022-09-15T21:56:10Z
  GitTreeState: clean
  GoVersion: go1.18
  Compiler: gc
  Platform: linux/amd64

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with argo-workflows installed or the issue environment reproducible.
